description: Non-BMP (supplementary-plane) cross-language canonical JSON conformance vectors. Keys must be sorted by UTF-16 code-unit sequence per RFC 8785 §3.2.3. All implementations must produce byte-for-byte identical canonical_hex and blake3_hex values. blake3_hex is raw BLAKE3 of the canonical bytes (no domain-separation prefix), matching Canonicalizer.get_hash() in Python and the equivalent in Rust/Go/JS. Generated from the Python reference implementation (protocol/canonical_json.py).
key_sort_note: UTF-16 and Unicode scalar order agree for U+0000–U+D7FF and U+E000–U+FFFF (BMP). They diverge for supplementary-plane characters (U+10000+): their surrogate pairs (0xD800–0xDFFF) sort *before* the upper BMP range U+E000–U+FFFF in UTF-16 order but *after* U+FFFF in scalar order. Implementations using scalar order produce different bytes for objects whose key sets straddle this boundary.

canonical_json_non_bmp.description: Single non-BMP key: U+10437 DESERET SMALL LETTER HU (𐐷). UTF-16 surrogate pair: 0xD801 0xDC37. Single-key object; confirms non-BMP keys round-trip through UTF-8 encoding correctly.
Single non-BMP key: U+1F980 CRAB EMOJI (🦀). UTF-16 surrogate pair: 0xD83E 0xDD80. Single-key object; confirms emoji keys round-trip through UTF-8 encoding correctly.
Mixed BMP/non-BMP keys. Input order: a, 𐐷, b, 🦀. Correct UTF-16 sort: a[0x0061] < b[0x0062] < 𐐷[0xD801,…] < 🦀[0xD83E,…]. Input is deliberately NOT in UTF-16 order to exercise the sort path.

Keys straddling the BMP/supplementary boundary: U+E000 (BMP private-use area), U+FFFD (replacement character), U+10000 (first supplementary character, 𐀀). Input is in scalar order (U+E000 < U+FFFD < U+10000). UTF-16 sort reverses U+10000: high surrogate 0xD800 < 0xE000 < 0xFFFD. This is the canonical divergence test between scalar and UTF-16 key ordering.
